_comment: Mnemosyne format sustainability registry. Tiers are ADVISORY and describe FORMAT longevity by two criteria only: (1) is the format publicly documented, and (2) are there multiple independent, healthy open-source readers? Vendor financial health is NOT a criterion. Tiers: OPEN, DOCUMENTED-PROPRIETARY, AT-RISK, UNKNOWN. This file is embedded in the binary; drop a formats.json in the data dir to override or extend it (entries are merged by extension, yours win).

.dng.rationale: Adobe DNG is publicly documented and TIFF/EP-based; read by many independent tools.

.tif.rationale: TIFF is an open, decades-stable spec with ubiquitous readers.

.tiff.rationale: TIFF is an open, decades-stable spec with ubiquitous readers.

.jpg.rationale: JPEG (ISO/IEC 10918) is a universal standard with many independent readers.

.jpeg.rationale: JPEG (ISO/IEC 10918) is a universal standard with many independent readers.

.png.rationale: PNG (ISO/IEC 15948, W3C) is an open spec with universal readers.

.gif.rationale: GIF spec is public and the LZW patent long expired; universal readers.

.bmp.rationale: BMP is a trivially documented bitmap format.

.webp.rationale: WebP has a public spec and a reference library (libwebp).

.svg.rationale: SVG is an open W3C XML vector format.

.pdf.rationale: PDF (ISO 32000) is an open spec with multiple independent readers.

.txt.rationale: Plain text has no format lock-in.

.md.rationale: Markdown is plain text with a simple, public convention.

.json.rationale: JSON is an open, plain-text data format.

.xml.rationale: XML is an open, plain-text markup standard.

.html.rationale: HTML is an open web standard.

.xmp.rationale: XMP is Adobe's publicly documented RDF/XML sidecar metadata.

.zip.rationale: ZIP has a public spec and universal readers.

.wav.rationale: WAV/RIFF PCM is open and trivially parseable.

.aiff.rationale: AIFF is an open, documented PCM audio container.

.flac.rationale: FLAC is an open spec with a reference decoder.

.mp3.rationale: MP3 is ubiquitous with many independent decoders; core patents have expired.

.mp4.rationale: MP4 (ISO base media file format) is an ISO standard read by many tools.

.nef.rationale: Nikon raw: the spec is closed, but libraw and dcraw decode it robustly.
.nef.migration: Retain the original NEF; consider archiving a DNG sibling for extra longevity.

.cr2.rationale: Canon raw (CR2): closed spec, decoded robustly by libraw/dcraw.
.cr2.migration: Retain the original CR2; consider archiving a DNG sibling.

.cr3.rationale: Canon raw (CR3): closed spec, decoded by recent libraw and exiftool.
.cr3.migration: Retain the original CR3; consider archiving a DNG sibling.

.arw.rationale: Sony raw: closed spec, decoded by libraw/dcraw.
.arw.migration: Retain the original ARW; consider archiving a DNG sibling.

.raf.rationale: Fujifilm raw: closed spec, decoded by libraw.
.raf.migration: Retain the original RAF; consider archiving a DNG sibling.

.orf.rationale: Olympus raw: closed spec, decoded by libraw.
.orf.migration: Retain the original ORF; consider archiving a DNG sibling.

.rw2.rationale: Panasonic raw: closed spec, decoded by libraw.
.rw2.migration: Retain the original RW2; consider archiving a DNG sibling.

.pef.rationale: Pentax raw: closed spec, decoded by libraw.
.pef.migration: Retain the original PEF; consider archiving a DNG sibling.

.3fr.rationale: Hasselblad raw: closed spec, decoded by libraw.
.3fr.migration: Retain the original; consider archiving a DNG sibling.

.psd.rationale: Photoshop document: closed spec, but read by GIMP, ImageMagick, and psd_tools.
.psd.migration: For long-term access, also export a flattened TIFF or PNG alongside the PSD.

.psb.rationale: Large Photoshop document: closed spec, partial open readers.
.psb.migration: Export a flattened TIFF alongside the PSB.

.heic.rationale: HEIF/HEVC (ISO/IEC 23008-12) is documented but HEVC is patent-encumbered; libheif decodes it.
.heic.migration: Consider archiving a JPEG or TIFF sibling for unencumbered access.

.heif.rationale: HEIF (ISO/IEC 23008-12) is documented but codec-encumbered; libheif decodes it.
.heif.migration: Consider archiving a JPEG or TIFF sibling.

.mov.rationale: QuickTime container: documented and widely read, but codecs may be encumbered.
.mov.migration: For long-term access, consider a widely-supported MP4/H.264 rendition.

.ai.rationale: Adobe Illustrator: proprietary, but modern .ai is PDF-compatible and readable.
.ai.migration: Export a PDF or SVG rendition for archival.

.docx.rationale: OOXML is a published standard but complex; LibreOffice reads it with good fidelity.
.docx.migration: Export a PDF for a fixed, archival rendition.

.xlsx.rationale: OOXML spreadsheet: published but complex; LibreOffice/openpyxl read it.
.xlsx.migration: Export CSV (data) and/or PDF (layout) for archival.

.lrcat.rationale: Lightroom catalog: a proprietary schema atop SQLite with no full independent reader. It is edit/metadata state, NOT your photos.
.lrcat.migration: Archive your ORIGINAL images and exported renditions; never rely on the catalog as the archive.

.cocatalogdb.rationale: Capture One catalog: proprietary, single-vendor, no independent reader.
.cocatalogdb.migration: Archive the original images and exports; the catalog is not the photos.

.cosessiondb.rationale: Capture One session: proprietary, single-vendor, no independent reader.
.cosessiondb.migration: Archive the original images and exports; the session is not the photos.

.aae.rationale: Apple photo-edit sidecar: Apple-specific non-destructive edit instructions with no general reader.
.aae.migration: Keep the original image; the .aae edits may not port outside Apple Photos.
